label: NATO Average Defense Spending (% GDP)
source: NATO Annual Report 2025 (released Mar 26, 2026); 19.6% surge in allied expenditure reported
deltaNote: vs. 2023 average of 2.53%; all 32 allies at 2%+ for first time

label: NATO Members Meeting 2% GDP Target
deltaNote: All 32 members meet 2% for first time in NATO history; up from 23 in 2025 and 3 in 2014

label: US Troops Deployed in Europe
contestNote: Partial drawdown from eastern flank announced Oct 2025; Poland and Lithuania say not notified

label: EU ReArm Europe Defense Fund
source: European Commission, ReArm Europe Plan, Mar 2025
contestNote: €650B is fiscal flexibility for member states, not direct EU spending; €150B is the binding SAFE loan instrument

label: Germany Defense Spending (% GDP)
deltaNote: First time Germany met 2% target since post-Cold War era; projected 3.5% by 2029

label: NATO Agreed Spending Target (by 2035)
contestNote: 3.5% for core defense + 1.5% for infrastructure/resilience; Spain and Belgium initially resisted

label: NATO Total Annual Defense Spending
deltaNote: Increase from 2023; represents ~55% of global military expenditure

label: Estimated European NATO Underinvestment Since 2014
contestNote: Methodology disputed; based on gap between actual spending and 2% target across all European members since Wales Pledge

label: NATO Member States
deltaNote: Finland (Apr 2023) and Sweden (Mar 2024) joined — largest expansion since 2004

label: Germany Special Defense Fund (Sondervermögen)
deltaNote: Original €100B fund from 2022 Zeitenwende expanded to €500B via constitutional amendment

label: US Share of NATO Defense Spending
contestNote: US counts Indo-Pacific and global commitments in spending; European analysts argue effective European contribution to European defense is higher

label: Rutte-Trump White House Meeting Outcome
source: CNN, Reuters, Washington Post, Trump Truth Social — Apr 8, 2026
delta: No withdrawal announced — but no reassurance given
deltaNote: 2-hour closed-door meeting Apr 8. Trump posted 'NATO WASN'T THERE WHEN WE NEEDED THEM' on Truth Social hours later. WaPo: 'Trump vents at NATO but avoids rupture.' Rutte said Trump 'clearly disappointed.'

label: Article 5 Reliability (US Commitment Status)
value: Institutional Crisis — EU Article 42.7 Blueprint Published; European Allies Back Spain; NATO: No Suspension Mechanism Exists
contestNote: Apr 25: Germany and Italy publicly backed Spain against the Pentagon email threats; NATO formally clarified its founding treaty has no mechanism to suspend or expel a member. Kallas released EU Article 42.7 operational blueprint — confirms it complements, not replaces, NATO. Apr 24: Pentagon email (Reuters) threatens Spain suspension, UK Falklands leverage, Rota base relocation; Hegseth 'get in a boat or get left behind.' Apr 18: Trump called NATO 'absolutely useless.' Stoltenberg: 'Not a natural law that NATO will exist for the next ten years.' Crisis has graduated from rhetorical conditionality to documented Pentagon contingency planning — partially checked by NATO's legal architecture and European bloc solidarity.

label: Republicans Who Say NATO Benefits US (Pew, Apr 2026)
deltaNote: Down from 49% in Apr 2025 — steepest single-year decline in Pew polling history; correlated with Iran war fallout and Trump's anti-NATO messaging. 82% of Democrats still say NATO benefits the US.

label: US-NATO Iran War Rift — Punitive Escalation
value: Punishment Drive Checked: European Solidarity + NATO Legal Firewall vs. Pentagon Contingency Planning
delta: Apr 28: NATO allies discussing ending annual summits to avoid Trump confrontations; Merz says Iran 'humiliating' Trump
deltaNote: Apr 28: Reuters reported NATO allies discussing abandoning annual summit format to avoid Trump confrontations — may skip 2028 summit entirely. Merz (Germany): Iran is 'humiliating' Trump administration but blockade is forcing negotiations. Apr 25–27: NATO issued three legal clarifications that its Washington Treaty has no suspension mechanism; Germany/Italy back Spain; EU Article 42.7 blueprint published. Apr 24: Pentagon email (Reuters) — Spain suspension, UK Falklands leverage, Rota relocation; Hegseth 'get in a boat or get left behind.' EU autonomous defense architecture accelerating. Next milestones: Helsingborg Foreign Ministers May 21–22; Ankara Summit July 7–8.

label: European Military Spending Total (SIPRI 2026)
source: SIPRI Global Military Spending Report 2026, released April 2026
deltaNote: Largest single-year European military spending increase since the Cold War. All 32 NATO members now meet or exceed 2% GDP target — second consecutive year. 22 of 29 European NATO members above 2%. Driven by German €108.2B 2026 budget, French military programming law, Polish 4%+, and Baltic states' sustained high-percentage commitments.
